processes.name: World Model Extraction
processes.description: Complete pipeline from raw input to validated world model
processes.trigger: User provides input text, code, or document

steps.action: Detect input source type from content patterns
Chunk input if it exceeds token limits
Extract entities, relations, processes, and constraints
Structure extraction into validated schema format
Validate integrity and compute quality score

outcomes: Structured world model with quality assessment

processes.name: Multi-Pass Enhancement
processes.description: Optional second pass to find implicit elements

steps.action: Analyze existing model for gaps
Structure delta into model format
Merge delta with existing model
Re-validate merged model

outcomes: Enhanced world model with implicit elements

processes.name: World Model Querying
processes.description: Answer questions about world model content

steps.action: Try deterministic graph pattern matching
If no pattern matches, use LLM inference
Format response with confidence and method

outcomes: Natural language answer with confidence score

processes.name: Model Refinement
processes.description: Incrementally update existing model with new information
processes.trigger: User provides new input for existing model

steps.action: Extract only new or changed elements
Structure delta into model format
Merge delta with existing model
Validate updated model

outcomes: Updated world model with new information integrated

processes.name: Model Transformation
processes.description: Apply natural language changes to world model

steps.action: Analyze transformation requirements
Generate entities/relations to add or modify
Apply changes to model
Re-validate transformed model

outcomes: Modified world model reflecting requested changes

processes.name: Model Export
processes.description: Convert world model to external formats

steps.action: Select appropriate export formatter
Transform model to target format
Write to file or stdout

outcomes: World model in requested format for external consumption

constraints.name: Token Limit Constraint
constraints.description: Input chunks must not exceed 80,000 tokens to fit in LLM context window

constraints.name: Schema Validation
constraints.description: All world models must conform to the defined Zod schema structure

constraints.name: Entity Reference Integrity
constraints.description: All relation sources/targets and process participants must reference valid entity IDs

constraints.name: Unique Entity IDs
constraints.description: Each entity within a world model must have a unique identifier

constraints.name: Process Step Ordering
constraints.description: Process steps should be ordered sequentially without gaps or duplicates

constraints.name: Retry Limit
constraints.description: LLM API calls are limited to 3 retry attempts with exponential backoff

constraints.name: Entity Name Deduplication
constraints.description: Entities with identical normalized names should be merged during model combination

constraints.name: Quality Score Range
constraints.description: Model quality scores must be between 0 and 100

constraints.name: Circular Dependency Detection
constraints.description: Models should not contain circular dependencies between entities

constraints.name: Confidence Score Range
constraints.description: All confidence scores must be between 0.0 and 1.0

constraints.name: API Timeout
constraints.description: LLM API calls must complete within 2 minutes

constraints.name: Non-Empty Extraction
constraints.description: Extractions should contain at least one entity or relation to be considered valid

metadata.source_summary: Analysis of SWM codebase revealing agent-based architecture for extracting, validating, and manipulating structured world models from various input sources
extraction_notes: Identified comprehensive multi-agent architecture with clear separation of concerns
System supports multiple input types and provides extensive CLI and programmatic interfaces
Strong validation and quality assurance throughout pipeline
Rich ecosystem of analysis, comparison, and export tools
MCP server integration enables live AI assistant interaction
Timeline and versioning support for model evolution tracking
Mathematical operations (algebra) on world models enable composition workflows

snapshots.label: v4 — tightened prompt, placeholder removal, 27 entities

model.name: SWM System Architecture
model.description: A comprehensive TypeScript system for extracting, structuring, analyzing, and serving world models from diverse inputs using LLM agents and graph analysis

entities.name: CLI Interface
entities.description: Command-line interface providing 20+ commands for building, inspecting, merging, and transforming world models

entities.name: Pipeline System
entities.description: Orchestrates the multi-stage world model extraction process with callbacks and progress tracking

entities.name: Extraction Agent
entities.description: LLM-powered agent that analyzes raw input and extracts entities, relations, processes, and constraints

entities.name: Structuring Agent
entities.description: Agent that converts raw LLM extractions into properly structured world models with normalized types and IDs

entities.name: Validation Agent
entities.description: Agent that performs integrity checks on world models, detecting orphans, dangling references, and circular dependencies

entities.name: Second Pass Agent
entities.description: Agent that finds implicit entities and relations missed in the first extraction pass

entities.name: Query Agent
entities.description: Agent that answers natural language questions about world models using graph patterns and LLM inference

entities.name: Refinement Agent
entities.description: Agent that incrementally updates existing world models with new input

entities.name: Transform Agent
entities.description: Agent that applies natural language transformations to modify world models

entities.name: Chunker Module
entities.description: Splits large inputs into token-sized chunks with paragraph-boundary awareness and overlap

entities.name: LLM Client
entities.description: Anthropic Claude API client with retry logic, timeout handling, and token estimation

entities.name: Graph Analysis Module
entities.description: Provides pathfinding, dependency analysis, impact analysis, clustering, and subgraph extraction

entities.name: World Model Schema
entities.description: Zod-based schema defining entities, relations, processes, constraints with validation

entities.name: MCP Server
entities.description: Model Context Protocol server exposing world model as 9 live queryable tools for AI agents

entities.name: Export System
entities.description: Converts world models to Claude MD, system prompts, MCP schemas, Mermaid diagrams, and DOT graphs

entities.name: Timeline System
entities.description: Tracks world model evolution over time with snapshots and automatic diffing

entities.name: Merge System
entities.description: Combines multiple world models with entity deduplication and ID remapping

entities.name: Fix System
entities.description: Auto-repairs world model validation issues like orphan entities and dangling references

entities.name: Entity
entities.description: Core domain object representing actors, systems, concepts, locations, events, groups, objects, or resources

entities.name: Relation
entities.description: Directed connection between entities with semantic types like depends_on, uses, contains

entities.name: Process
entities.description: Dynamic sequence of ordered steps with actors, triggers, and outcomes

entities.name: Constraint
entities.description: Invariant rule that must hold true, with hard or soft severity

entities.name: Prompt Templates
entities.description: Specialized extraction prompts for different source types (text, code, conversation, document, URL)

entities.name: File Input
entities.description: Text files, code files, JSON, YAML, CSV, and other document formats

entities.name: URL Input
entities.description: Web pages fetched and stripped of HTML for content extraction

entities.name: Validation Result
entities.description: Comprehensive validation report with issues, stats, and quality scores

entities.name: Query Result
entities.description: Answer to natural language questions with method, confidence, and referenced entities

processes.name: World Model Extraction
processes.description: Complete pipeline to extract structured world models from any input type
processes.trigger: User runs swm model command with input

steps.action: Detect source type from file extension or content patterns
Check input size and chunk if necessary
Extract entities, relations, processes, constraints using specialized prompts
Structure raw extraction into proper world model with ID generation
Validate model integrity and generate quality score

outcomes: Structured world model with validation report

processes.name: Multi-Pass Extraction
processes.description: Enhanced extraction with multiple passes to find implicit elements

steps.action: Run standard extraction pipeline
Find implicit entities and relations missed in first pass
Structure delta extraction
Merge original and delta models
Final validation of merged result

outcomes: More complete world model with implicit elements captured

processes.name: Natural Language Query
processes.description: Answer questions about world models using graph patterns and LLM inference
processes.trigger: User asks question via swm query command

steps.action: Try to match question against known graph patterns
If pattern matched, execute graph analysis
If no pattern match, use LLM inference with model context
Format answer with method, confidence, and referenced entities

outcomes: Natural language answer with confidence and method

processes.name: MCP Tool Execution
processes.description: Serve world model as live tools for AI agents via Model Context Protocol

steps.action: Parse tool call parameters
Execute appropriate graph analysis or query
Format result for AI agent consumption

outcomes: Real-time domain expertise for AI agents

constraints.name: Token Limit Constraint
constraints.description: Input chunks must not exceed 80,000 tokens to fit within Claude's context window

constraints.name: Entity Name Uniqueness
constraints.description: Entity names within a world model should be unique after normalization

constraints.name: Relation Reference Integrity
constraints.description: All relation source and target IDs must reference existing entities

constraints.name: Process Step Ordering
constraints.description: Process steps must have monotonically increasing order numbers

constraints.name: Schema Compliance
constraints.description: All world models must validate against the Zod schema

constraints.name: API Rate Limiting
constraints.description: Claude API calls must respect rate limits with exponential backoff retry

constraints.name: Source Type Detection
constraints.description: Input must be classified into one of six source types for appropriate prompt selection

constraints.name: Confidence Scoring
constraints.description: All extractions must include confidence scores between 0 and 1

metadata.source_summary: Analysis of complete SWM codebase including CLI, agents, pipeline, schema, utilities, and server components
extraction_notes: System implements sophisticated multi-pass extraction with chunk handling
Strong emphasis on validation and auto-fixing of model integrity issues
Provides both CLI and MCP server interfaces for different use cases
Graph analysis capabilities include pathfinding, clustering, and impact analysis
Export system supports multiple formats for AI consumption (Claude MD, system prompts, MCP schemas)

diff_from_previous.summary: +7 entities, -40 entities, ~18 entities modified, +14 relations, -23 relations, +3 processes, +6 constraints

model.name: SWM v1.0.0
model.description: Structured World Model system — the universal lens

entities.name: SWM System
entities.description: Main structured world model extraction system that converts any input into a complete architectural model

entities.name: CLI Interface
entities.description: Command-line interface that provides 30+ commands for building, inspecting, querying, and manipulating world models

entities.name: Pipeline System
entities.description: Configurable pipeline that orchestrates the multi-stage world model extraction process with callbacks and error handling

entities.name: Extraction Agent
entities.description: LLM-powered agent that analyzes raw input and extracts entities, relations, processes, and constraints using source-specific prompts

entities.name: Structuring Agent
entities.description: Agent that converts raw extraction output into valid WorldModel schema, handling ID generation and entity deduplication

entities.name: Validation Agent
entities.description: Comprehensive validation system that checks model integrity, detects orphans, cycles, and computes quality scores 0-100

entities.name: Second Pass Agent
entities.description: Completeness agent that finds implicit entities and relations missed by the first extraction pass

entities.name: Refinement Agent
entities.description: Incremental extraction agent that updates existing models with new input while preserving existing knowledge

entities.name: Query Agent
entities.description: Natural language query system with 10 graph patterns for deterministic queries plus LLM fallback for open-ended questions

entities.name: Transform Agent
entities.description: Agent that applies natural language transformations to world models, adding/removing/modifying elements

entities.name: Chunking System
entities.description: Text chunking system that splits large inputs into LLM-compatible chunks with overlap preservation and paragraph boundaries

entities.name: WorldModel Schema
entities.description: Zod-based schema defining entities, relations, processes, constraints with 8 entity types and 17 relation types

entities.name: Graph Operations
entities.description: Graph analysis utilities providing dependency tracking, path finding, subgraph extraction, cluster detection, and impact analysis

entities.name: Merge System
entities.description: World model composition system that merges, diffs, and applies algebraic operations (intersection, difference, overlay)

entities.name: Export System
entities.description: Multi-format export system supporting Claude Markdown, system prompts, MCP schemas, and Mermaid diagrams

entities.name: MCP Server
entities.description: Model Context Protocol server that exposes world model as live queryable tools for AI agents

entities.name: Anthropic Claude
entities.description: External LLM service used for all extraction, validation, and inference operations with retry logic and timeout handling

entities.name: Timeline System
entities.description: Version tracking system that captures model evolution over time with automatic diff computation

entities.name: Fix System
entities.description: Auto-repair system that resolves validation issues like orphan entities, dangling references, and duplicates

entities.name: Coverage System
entities.description: Model comparison system that measures how much of a reference model is covered by another model

entities.name: Source Type Detector
entities.description: Heuristic system that analyzes input content to classify as text, code, document, URL, conversation, or mixed

entities.name: Entity
entities.description: Core model element representing actors, objects, systems, concepts, locations, events, groups, or resources

entities.name: Relation
entities.description: Directed edge between entities with semantic types like depends_on, uses, produces, controls

entities.name: Process
entities.description: Dynamic sequence with ordered steps, participants, triggers, and outcomes representing workflows

entities.name: Constraint
entities.description: Invariant or rule with hard/soft severity that must hold true for specific entities

entities.name: JSON Schema Generator
entities.description: System that converts Zod schemas to JSON Schema for validation and code generation

relations.label: provides command-line access to all system functionality
merges new extractions with existing model

processes.name: World Model Extraction
processes.description: Multi-stage pipeline that converts raw input into structured world model
processes.trigger: User provides input text, code, or document

steps.action: Detect source type and chunk large inputs
Extract entities, relations, processes, constraints using LLM
Convert to valid schema with ID generation and deduplication
Validate integrity and compute quality score

outcomes: Complete structured world model with validation report

processes.name: Multi-Pass Extraction
processes.description: Enhanced extraction with second pass to find implicit elements

steps.action: Run standard extraction pipeline
Analyze first pass results and find missing implicit elements
Merge delta with existing model
Final validation of merged result

outcomes: More complete world model with inferred elements

processes.name: Model Query
processes.description: Process natural language questions about world model

steps.action: Try deterministic graph pattern matching
If no pattern match, use LLM inference
Extract referenced entities from answer

outcomes: Natural language answer with method and confidence

processes.name: Model Merge
processes.description: Combine two world models into unified representation
processes.trigger: User requests merge of two models

steps.action: Deduplicate entities by normalized name
Remap all IDs and merge relations/processes/constraints
Validate merged result

processes.name: MCP Service
processes.description: Serve world model as live queryable tools for AI agents

steps.action: Load world model from file
Register 9 tools for entity lookup, relations, queries, etc.
Listen for tool calls and route to appropriate handlers

constraints.name: LLM Dependency
constraints.description: System requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and network access to function

constraints.name: Schema Validation
constraints.description: All world models must conform to WorldModel Zod schema

constraints.name: Entity Reference Integrity
constraints.description: All relation source/target IDs must reference existing entities

constraints.name: Chunk Size Limit
constraints.description: Individual chunks cannot exceed 80,000 tokens for LLM processing

constraints.name: Quality Score Range
constraints.description: Quality scores must be between 0-100

constraints.name: Process Step Ordering
constraints.description: Process steps should be in ascending order

constraints.name: Entity Name Uniqueness
constraints.description: Entity names should be unique within a model to avoid confusion

constraints.name: Confidence Range
constraints.description: Entity and extraction confidence values must be between 0.0-1.0

constraints.name: MCP Tool Limit
constraints.description: MCP server exposes exactly 9 predefined tools

constraints.name: Multi-Pass Limit
constraints.description: Maximum 3 extraction passes to prevent excessive processing

metadata.source_summary: TypeScript codebase with 26 source files implementing multi-agent extraction pipeline, graph operations, export formats, and CLI interface
extraction_notes: Extracted from comprehensive TypeScript codebase showing sophisticated multi-agent architecture
System demonstrates clear separation of concerns with specialized agents for each phase
Strong emphasis on validation, quality scoring, and error recovery
Extensible design supports multiple input types and output formats
MCP integration enables AI agent interoperability
